calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2021 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant University Of San Franciscos Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-16-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
John Strouss, Esq., Law Offices of John P. Strouss, III, 415-771-6174, for Plaintiff, Kira Papineau.
Robert Trodella, Esq. Trodella & Laping LLP, 415-381-1280, for Defendant, University of San Francisco,
Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 Court reporter.

Defendant USF seeks clarification of the Court's tentative ruing.

Matter is discussed and Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Defendant University of San Francisco's summary judgment motion is denied.

Plaintiff Kira Papaneau was a USF student when she slipped and fell on a classroom building stairway and sued for premises negligence. USF's motion asserts that "Papaneau cannot establish breach of duty or causation because she admits that she did not observe an unsafe condition on the step where she slipped."

However, USF concedes "summary judgment does not commonly lend itself to resolving a negligence case" and that is so here. Papaneau demonstrates triable issues of material fact with her testimony that steps below the one she fell on were wet, coupled with a meteorologist's report that it had rained at USF that day. (Papaneau Dep. 146; Clark Dec. 2:20-3:2.) Papaneau also adduces opinions from a safety expert, who notes the absence of mats and signs near the stairs and unsafe slip-and-fall circumstances caused by the stairs' configuration. (Ferrari Dec.; see also Papaneau Dep. 200.) As to notice, "[w]hether a dangerous condition has existed long enough for a reasonable person to have discovered it is a question of fact for the jury." (Ortega v. Kmart Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1200. 1207.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And To Impose Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-16-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM:
Robert Trodella (415-399-1015) of Trodella & Lapping LLP for Defendant.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court notes the tentative ruling was not contested and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION to Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And To Impose Monetary Sanctions:

Defendant's motion is granted part and denied in part. Denied is the motion to deem Requests for Admissions as admitted. Plaintiff served substantially compliant verified responses prior to the hearing on the motion. The defendant is entitled to the verified response in proper pleading format. Defendant points out service of a verification which is not attached to the responsive pleading presents logistical and procedural difficulties if the pleading is presented as evidence at trial. Plaintiff shall within fifteen days of the date of this Order to re-serve her verified response to Request for Admissions, Set One. A monetary sanction for failure to timely serve a substantially compliant verified response to Request for Admissions until after filing of a motion but prior to the hearing is mandatory. See C.C.P. 2033.220 (c). Plaintiff's counsel is sanctioned in the sum of $660.00 to paid within the aforesaid fifteen days.

Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling via email to sbs@sbslawsf.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steve Stein, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
